title: Job Development * Blindness - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Job Development * Blindness 5 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - Adults With Disabilities/Health Conditions Blindness Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF OREGON COMMISSION FOR THE BLIND EUGENE FIELD OFFICE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides vocational rehabilitation, independent living skills training, career exploration and general counseling for legally blind adults. Also provides independent living services for visually impaired individuals. Includes orientation and mobility training, techniques of daily living, Braille, counseling, support groups, vocational training, job development, and transition services for blind high school students. Also offers a selection of adaptive items for people with visual impairments. 541 Willamette Avenue #408 Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Varies by service; adults certified legally blind or diagnosed with condition leading to blindness, or age 55 years and above and unable to drive or read newspaper with glasses. Age 55 and older for independent living services. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 686-7990 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON COMMISSION FOR THE BLIND MEDFORD FIELD OFFICE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides vocational rehabilitation, independent living skills training, career exploration and general counseling for legally blind adults.
